inlineContent.text: iOS keyboards use a
to integrate with the currently selected text field. The proxy lets you insert and delete text, get the currently selected text, move the input cursor, etc.

The native APIs are however very limited, which makes it hard to get detailed text information and to perform many standard operations. For instance, the native proxy can’t get all the text from the document, and lacks many important capabilities.
KeyboardKit therefore extends the
with additional capabilities.
also has a custom
that lets you hijack the original proxy with a custom one.
👑 KeyboardKit Pro unlocks ways for
to read the full document content to all licenses that includes the
feature. You can sign up for KeyboardKit Pro from the
KeyboardKit has a
namespace with proxy-related types, like
and
KeyboardKit extends the native
with additional capabilities, such as the ability to get more content from the document, analyze words, sentences & quotations, end the current sentence, etc.
Here are some examples of extensions that are added to the proxy by KeyboardKit, to make it much more capable than it is by default:
See the
documentation for more information and a full list of extension that are applied by KeyboardKit.
KeyboardKit Pro unlocks additional proxy capabilities, like the ability to read the
instead of just the content closest to the input cursor.

Apple’s native text document proxy doesn’t return all the text within the document. It instead cuts off the available text at any time, e.g. at the previous or next paragraph. This makes it hard to provide AI-based features that require more context.
KeyboardKit Pro therefore unlocks
proxy extensions that let you read
all
text from the proxy. This will provide you with all the text before and after the input cursor, as well as an aggregate of all the available text in the document.
The proxy also has
functions if you’re only interested in the text before or after the input cursor.

The full document read operation is a best effort operation, that makes the most of the very limited capabilities that are provided by the operating system. It’s not guaranteed to return a valid result. You can configure the operation to yield better results, for instance by waiting longer when moving the input cursor, try to move more times around the edges, etc.
